# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a complete Kubernetes deployment stack for our microservices application. Include Deployment, Service, and Ingress resources. Deploy to the default namespace for simplicity. We need this running quickly for our demo to stakeholders next week.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

```yaml
apiVersion: v1  # Kubernetes API version used in YAML file (v1) or if you're using a different tool/library for managing resources, use the appropriate one here e.g., apps/v1beta2 when dealing with custom resource definitions and controllers like Deployment
kind: Pod    # Kind of Kubernetes object we are creating - in this case it is 'Pod'. This should match what you're using to interact directly with your resources on the cluster (e.g., kubectl, minikube/kops etc.) 
metadata:   # Metadata for our resource that includes name and namespace of Pods or other Kubernetes objects in this case 'my-microservice'. This is required by default but can be overridden if needed (e.g., kubectl create --namespace=<NAMESPACE> pod/foo)
  labels:    # Label keys are used to select resources and organize them based on their characteristics, in this case 'app' with value of microservice-name ('microservices') for all the Pods created from our application. (e.g., kubectl label --namespace=<NAMESPACE> pod/foo app=my_application)
    app: my_application  # Label key is "app" and its value will be 'my-micoservice' for all the Pods created from our application in this namespace. (e.g., kubectl label --namespace=$NAMESPACE pod/foo app=microservices)
spec:   # Specification of what should happen when we create or update a resource - here, it is to run an image called 'my-image' on the Pods created from our application in this namespace. (e.g., kubectl run --namespace=$NAMESPACE my_application/1234567890:v1)
  containers:   # Containers are part of a pod and they can contain different resources like CPU, memory etc which the Pod is using (e.g., kubectl exec --namespace=$NAMESPACE my_application/1234567890 cat /proc/meminfo)
  - name: microservices   # Name of container in pod ('microservice') and image to use for the Pods created from our application. (e., kubectl run --namespace=$NAMESPACE my_application/1234567890 cat /proc/meminfo)
    image: microservices   # Docker Image name ('microservice') that should be used for the Pods created from our application. (e., kubectl run --namespace=$NAMESPACE my
